result:
By default, React re-renders every child when the parent re-renders — regardless of whether props changed. That's the model, not a bug.

To skip re-renders when props are shallow-equal:

```jsx
const Child = React.memo(function Child(props) { ... });
```

Gotchas that defeat `memo`:
- **New object/array/function props each render.** `<Child onClick={() => ...} />` or `<Child style={{...}} />` creates a fresh reference every time → memo sees "changed" → re-renders. Wrap with `useCallback` / `useMemo`, or hoist the literal.
- **Children as JSX.** `<Child><Thing/></Child>` passes a new `children` element each parent render.
- **Context.** A memoized component still re-renders when a consumed context value changes.

Quick diagnosis: open React DevTools → Profiler → "Why did this render?". It tells you which prop's identity changed.

If the child is cheap, don't bother — `memo` isn't free. Only reach for it when the Profiler shows it matters.
